justifications.ip1: All three ACs have specific failing test scenarios: AC1 covered by T1 (asserts `callArgs.channel === '#aml-alerts'`) and T2 (alertId/amount in message), AC2 by T3 (asserts `callArgs.to === 'compliance@enterprise.com'` and exact subject format), AC3 by T5/T6/T6b (Slack 5xx returns `{slack:'failed',email:'sent'}`, never throws, both-fail returns `{slack:'failed',email:'failed'}`).
justifications.ip2: The plan creates only `src/compliance/alert-router.js` and `tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js` as specified, explicitly states 'No SAR/MLRO logic (out of scope)' in the handoff checklist, and the architecture constraint (`src/audit/audit-logger.js`, not `console.log`) is enforced via the injected `auditLogger` parameter with tests T7ãT8c.
justifications.ip3: Every test names the exact function (`createAlertRouter`, `routeAlert`), the specific files (`src/compliance/alert-router.js`, `tests/compliance/alert-router.test.js`), and concrete assertions (e.g. `callArgs.channel === '#aml-alerts'`, `callArgs.subject === 'AML Threshold Breach ã ALERT_003'`, `auditLoggerStub.log.calledOnce === true`).
justifications.ip4: The plan preserves test-first order throughout ã the full test suite is written before any implementation code ã but the structure presents the entire RED phase test file followed by the entire GREEN phase implementation file rather than per-task RED/run-to-see-RED/GREEN steps, omitting the explicit 'run tests to see RED' confirmation between individual test groups.
justifications.ip5: NFR-1 (audit logging) has four dedicated test scenarios (T7 confirms `auditLoggerStub.log.calledOnce`, T8 asserts `logEntry.alertId` and `channelStatus`, T8b checks `timestamp`, T8c verifies logging even when both channels fail), and the architecture constraint (use `src/audit/audit-logger.js`, not `console.log`) is enforced as a task-level implementation rule via dependency injection.

notes: Exceptionally thorough plan with concrete assertions for all ACs and NFR-1, full out-of-scope discipline, and precise file/function naming; minor deduction on IP4 for presenting the RED phase as a monolithic file dump rather than per-task RED-run-GREEN cycles with explicit mid-task 'confirm RED' steps.
